subject: Re: Unbinned tomogram dimensions in Reconstruct tomograms
body:
Thank you for your patient reply!

在2021年6月1日星期二 UTC+8 下午9:50:19<alist...@gmail.com> 写道：

> Hi Yuqi,
>
> Warp doesn’t know anything about whether your data is super-resolution or 
> not, you just tell it the pixel size, a binning factor and a set of 
> tomogram dimensions to reconstruct. The tomogram dimensions will always be 
> in relation to the unbinned pixel size.
>
> If you look at your super-resolution images with something like ‘header’ 
> from IMOD you will see they have 8k x 6k (super-resolution) pixels. This is 
> the data warp sees when reconstructing, you have to specify your tomogram 
> dimensions relative to this pixel spacing.
>
> To answer your specific example
>
> K3 data, physical pixel size 2.10A, SR pixel size 1.05A
> Raw tilt-images, 8k x 6k @1.05Apx
> Tomogram, unbinned dimensions 6k x 8k x thickness
> Tomogram, binned dimensions = (unbinned dimensions) / 2^binning factor
>
> e.g. tomogram bin1 = (6k x 8k x thickness) / 2^1 = 3k x 4k x thickness/2
>
> You only need to generate downsampled tomograms in Warp, you don’t need 
> reconstructions at anything higher than 10A/px or so
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On 1 Jun 2021, at 13:29, yuqi zhang <8245...@qq.com> wrote:
>
> If so, when I process the K3 camera datasets(4000*3000), the pixel size 
> is, for example, 1.05A (super resolution). How can I specify the parameter? 
> 1.05A/1.05A, bin1, 6000*8000*thickness. Is it OK?
> 在2021年6月1日星期二 UTC+8 下午6:14:47<Pranav Shah> 写道：
>
>> yes. binning in warp occurs at 2^n... so 2^1 means bin2 and 2^0 means 
>> no binning... 
>> Best, 
>> Pranav 
>> -- 
>> Pranav Shah 
>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow. 
>>
>> Division of Structural Biology, 
>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics, 
>> University of Oxford, 
>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN, 
>> UK 
>>
>> On Tue, Jun 1, 2021 at 9:42 AM yuqi zhang <8245...@qq.com> wrote: 
>> > 
>> > I found a difference between Warp and IMOD reconstruction. If I used 
>> 0.675/0.675A, bin1,4000*4000*3000 in Warp. The resultof size was half of 
>> IMOD reconstruction. And I tried 8000*8000*6000, it had a right size. 
>> Maybe, does the "Unbinned" mean the super resolution? 
>> >
